title: CBS News
rights: public domain (presumed; pre-1963 broadcast, no rights statement on archive.org item)
transcript: Do know the time folks have again, so I'll just say right now it can. 10 o'clock, 10 o'clock, 10 o'clock. Correct, mountain time. 10 o'clock, courtesy, copy jewelry. For over 30 years, famous for diamonds. Fire transcription. Here's Robert Trout, with five minutes of the latest news, brought to you by the four dealers of your community. America's worth more cars, the new 53 Ford. Ford. With its quality-built crest-marked body, this Ford is setting style standards for years to come. And Ford's new Runder Ride is another worth-more feature designed to make this Ford worth more today and years from today. Ford's new Runder Ride combines advances like more responsive spring and shock absorber action with new rubber compression bumpers to reduce front-end road shock up to 80%. Visit your Ford dealers, test-drive the 53-4 tomorrow. Now you're a nightly reporter, Robert Trump. Soviet Russia tonight broke off diplomatic relations with Israel, demanding that the Israeli minister leave Moscow without delay. And the Russians, of course, are withdrawing their mission from Tel Aviv. It was the Soviet negation in Tel Aviv that was bombed under this week by Hebrew extremists. With the premiere of Israel, David Ben-Gurion calling the bombing a senseless crime and offering the compensate Russia for the damage. That Moscow accused the Israeli officials of conniving in the incident, and not tonight we have the breaking off of diplomatic relations between the two countries. We can expect a great uproar from the iron curtain countries in protest over President Eisenhower's decision that the atomic spies must die, but practically all of the action that we have so far tonight is in support of the president. Republican Senator Robert Taft says, I don't see how President Eisenhower could do anything else. And Democratic Senator Henry Jackson of Washington State who used to be a member of the The atomic energy committee in Congress says he can curse with what the president said about the seriousness of the offense committed by Julius Ametha Rosenberg and the obvious fact that their action in giving atomic secrets to Russia could be disastrous to thousands and perhaps millions of people. The Supreme Court will be asked once again by the Rosenberg's attorney to review the case which the court previously refused to do, and that seems to be the last hope left for the Rosenberg's who have heard the news of President Eisenhower's decision on radio's in their cells at Sing Sing that gave no outward sign of emotion. But really for a month, the Federal Jewelry here in New York has been hearing the case of John Perrull, the former Army sergeant accused of treason for helping the Japanese during the Second World War, and a few minutes ago, the Jewelry returned to verdict guilty of treason on two counts. President Eisenhower and his State of the Union message called for the rule of the Reciprocal Trade Act, and today the President conferred with Republican Congressional leaders for an an hour-and-a-half discussing the general subject of international trade. The domestic economy came in for attention today when the President named James Brownlee the New York investment banker to the job of government consultant on wage and price controls. Assigned perhaps that the new administration is thinking in terms of keeping some curbs on wages and prices possibly on a standby basis. In time the decontrolling process goes on, the United Press says that tomorrow the government It may do away with the price ceilings on poetry eggs, teen soap, and lard. Chairman Salton Stoll of the Senate Armed Services Committee says the General James Van Fleet, the former commander of the 8th Army in Korea, will be asked to explain his statement that an allied offensive now can break the Korean stalemate. Senators of quoted General Omar Bradley as saying that such an attack would cost many lives and would not assure us an early victory at all. In Korea tonight, the South Korean President, Mr. Singh Mri, endorsed the proposals for a blockade of the coast of China, and in East Germany today, the Communist began talking all over again about a new blockade of Berlin. Tomorrow night, at this time, Secretary of State John Foster-Dulles will make a radio report to the American People and this program will not be heard tomorrow, but I will of course be back with the news as usual on Friday night. This is Robert Trout in New York. Now here is Harry Clark for the Ford dealer in New York Community. To save on truck running costs, get a thrifty new Ford truck. Only Ford trucks offer savings up to 14% on gas. Ford's modern, all-new overhead valve short stroke engines reduce piston travel, cut friction, and save up to one gallon in seven. Only Ford trucks give you all this plus a choice of V8 or 6 engines. See your Ford dealer now for a thrifty new Ford truck. He'll give you a generous allowance on your old truck. Listen for Robert Krauton, Friday evening on the CBS Radio Network. I'm CBS Radio, serving the Midland Empire, K. Double O.K. and Billing. How did it sign for Sheridan Bear? Wherever you go, people are stopping for Sheridan Bear. Because of its mellow brewing, it is truly... for premium beer out of North West. ["Fantasy of the World"] ["Fantasy of the World"] Everybody on your twinkling star Doesn't matter on which one you are If you're digging me on your radar Hello, how's the hello? You are a strange and foreign race If you are equipped to fly through space Pay a little visit to our place Hello, how's the hello? If you got nothing else to do Just grab up the rocket To pocket to pocket to pocket if you've a pair of wings that you attached to was pocket Don't you let a bear us worry you we are pretty funny looking to Anywhere in case of come Hello Are you listening well hello out there get ready to go This is quick viewing, ready to conduct another recorded clips tour through music lines. I'm going to find the stoppops in the lamp today and they're all for you during the next 55 minutes. So, tune in my radar set and let's get going. At 31 degrees, it's cooked on this chilly snowy February night. We begin with Percy Faith on the orchestra playing the sweetest rap music.
